seo.title: Llama Stack Guardrails for Government
seo.description: Compliance guide for Llama Stack agents in government. Meet FedRAMP, FISMA requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Government teams deploying Llama Stack face stringent oversight from FedRAMP, FISMA, and NIST 800-53, requiring robust safeguards against AI risks in sensitive environments. Compliance guardrails are essential to prevent national security breaches, ensure explainable decisions, and maintain public trust. Vex provides verifiable compliance layers that integrate seamlessly with Llama Stack's agentic workflows.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires continuous monitoring and auditing of cloud-based AI systems handling federal data.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex logs all agent turns with verifiable audit trails via vex.verify(), enabling FedRAMP-compliant monitoring of Llama Stack safety shield outputs.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates risk assessments for AI systems, including controls for access, integrity, and hallucination prevention.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces schema validation on tool responses and memory retrievals, blocking non-compliant Llama Stack outputs before they propagate.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires algorithmic impact assessments and explainability for AI decisions affecting citizens.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex captures reasoning traces from Llama Stack agent loops, generating audit-ready explanations for every decision path.

complianceRequirements.description: Ensures AI outputs are accessible and usable by individuals with disabilities.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates Llama Stack responses against WCAG 2.1 accessibility schemas, flagging non-compliant content.

industryRisks.name: False Positive Safety Filtering
industryRisks.description: Llama Stack safety shield blocks benign policy documents as 'sensitive content', halting legitimate workflows.
industryRisks.example: Veterans Affairs agent rejects FOIA request processing due to false positive on 'classified' keywords.

industryRisks.name: Agent Loop Exhaustion
industryRisks.description: Turn limits exceeded without resolution, causing stalled citizen service requests.
industryRisks.example: Social Security benefits inquiry loops indefinitely on edge cases, violating FISMA response time requirements.

industryRisks.name: Tool Schema Violations
industryRisks.description: External API tools return malformed JSON, breaking downstream government integrations.
industryRisks.example: Treasury payment verification tool returns invalid schema, risking erroneous fund transfers.

industryRisks.name: Memory Hallucinations
industryRisks.description: Retrieval associates incorrect documents, leading to misinformed policy decisions.
industryRisks.example: DOJ case management hallucinates classified evidence associations, breaching handling protocols.

implementation.title: Verify Safety Shield Outputs
implementation.description: Wrap Llama Stack agent responses to catch false positives while maintaining audit trails.
implementation.code:
import vex

from llama_stack_client import LlamaStackAgent

agent = LlamaStackAgent()

@ vex.verify({
    'safety_filter': {'allowlist': ['policy', 'foia', 'regulation']},
    'audit': 'gov-fedramp',
    'max_turns': 10
})
def process_citizen_request(prompt):
    response = agent.run(prompt)
    if 'safety_blocked' in response:
        vex.log('false_positive_check', response)
    return response

result = process_citizen_request('Process FOIA request for public records')

implementation.title: Schema Validation for Tools
implementation.description: Ensure Llama Stack tool calls conform to government API schemas before execution.

implementation.title: Memory Retrieval Guardrails
implementation.description: Prevent hallucinated document associations in Llama Stack memory bank.
implementation.code:
import vex
from llama_stack_client import MemoryBank

memory = MemoryBank()

@vex.verify({
    'document_hash': True,
    'classification_check': ['unclassified', 'foia'],
    'max_associations': 3,
    'gov_policy': 'no_classified_external_llm'
})
def safe_retrieve(query):
    docs = memory.search(query)
    vex.log_evidence('memory_trace', docs)
    return [d for d in docs if d.classification != 'classified']

results = safe_retrieve('social security policy 2024')

caseStudyScenario.heading: Llama Stack in Government: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: The Department of Veterans Affairs deploys Llama Stack to automate FOIA request processing across 50 regional offices. Agents handle document classification, redaction recommendations, and response drafting using integrated memory banks containing policy manuals and case law. During peak volume, the safety shield begins flagging standard VA policy terms like 'veteran status' and 'benefits tier' as sensitive, causing 40% of requests to fail silently. Tool calls to the records management API return inconsistent schemas, breaking the approval workflow. Memory retrieval hallucinates associations between unrelated benefits policies, generating incorrect redaction suggestions that expose PII.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex guardrails reduced false positives by 92% through allowlisted government terminology and schema enforcement. Agent loop monitoring prevented exhaustion by early termination of non-convergent turns, maintaining sub-2-minute response times required by FISMA. Every decision became auditable with embedded reasoning traces, satisfying Executive Order explainability requirements. The deployment passed its FedRAMP assessment on first review, avoiding 18 months of remediation delays.

cta.heading: Comply with Government Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex with Llama Stack today to achieve FedRAMP-ready AI deployments.
